description: Psychology of comments, shares, saves, and follows — what triggers each behavior

rules.title: Debate/Opinion Triggers Comments
rules.description: Content with a debatable opinion or controversial statement generates 4.8x more comments than neutral content. The brain is compelled to respond to perceived wrongness. 'Hot take:' and 'Unpopular opinion:' prefixes are proven triggers. Comments boost distribution 2.1x.

rules.title: Identity Validation Triggers Shares
rules.description: Content that makes viewers feel understood or validates their identity gets shared 3.6x more. 'Tag someone who…' and 'This is literally me' moments are the highest-share triggers. Viewers share to express identity, not just to inform.

rules.title: Utility Value Triggers Saves
rules.description: Content with actionable utility ('save this for later') gets saved 5.2x more than entertainment content. Viewers save to reference: recipes, tutorials, tips, templates, checklists. Explicit 'save this' CTA in captions increases save rate 67%.

rules.title: Future Value Promise Triggers Follows
rules.description: Ending content with a specific future value promise ('Follow for part 2', 'I post [niche] every [day]') converts 3.1x more followers than no CTA. The viewer must understand exactly what value they'll receive. Vague CTAs ('follow for more') convert 78% less.

rules.title: Awe and Wonder: Most Viral Share Emotion
rules.description: Awe (vastness, beauty, unexpected scale) is the #1 emotion driving viral shares, outperforming humor (2.3x), anger (1.8x), and sadness (3.1x) in share rate. Nature, space, unexpected skill, and radical acts of kindness trigger awe most reliably.

rules.title: Reward at the End Drives Completion
rules.description: Videos that promise and deliver a payoff (reveal, punchline, result, surprise) at the end have 2.6x higher completion rate. The payoff must match the hook's promise. Broken promises (clickbait) destroy trust: 73% of disappointed viewers never return.

rules.title: Social Proof Numbers Stop Scroll
rules.description: Showing specific engagement numbers ('12M people watched this', '500K saves') in the hook creates FOMO-driven curiosity. Viewers need to know why so many others engaged. Specificity matters: '12.4M' outperforms 'millions' by 34%.

rules.title: Nostalgia: Highest Share Rate for 25-40 Demographic
rules.description: Nostalgia content (90s/2000s references, childhood memories, 'remember when') generates the highest share rates for 25-40 age group (avg 9.2% share rate). Shared to reconnect with peers from that era. Platform-agnostic.
